text.title: EU Sovereign LLM Deployment Bundle Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of eu-sovereign-llm-deployment-bundle enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Inference, vector store, audit log, and observability stack ALL live in EEA regions; sub-processors verified end-to-end.
rule.rationale: A single non-EEA hop breaks sovereignty regardless of intent.

rule.statement: Egress is deny-by-default at the network layer; allowed destinations are an explicit allow-list reviewed quarterly.
rule.rationale: Default-allow egress is the dominant data-leak vector in cloud LLM workloads.

rule.statement: Every inference call writes an AI-Act audit event (model_id, version, inputs_hash, outputs_hash, user_id, decision_class).
rule.rationale: Article 13 + Article 12 require traceability; building audit at the gateway is the only sustainable place.

rule.statement: Risk classification is re-evaluated whenever inputs, model, or downstream impact change.
rule.rationale: Risk class drifts as products evolve; stale classification = wrong controls.

rule.statement: Sub-processor chain is enumerated and contractually limited to EEA + adequacy-decision countries.
rule.rationale: Hidden US sub-processor = hidden Schrems-II exposure.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, skip this methodology and route to the adjacent one referenced in Related.
rule.rationale: A skip rule keeps the decision tree closed: every branch ends at a real rule id.
